formatted_summary:
agent1 (Round 2 summarized): Landolt C charts may offer better detection sensitivity than Snellen charts in strabismic amblyopia because they rely less on visual crowding effects common during binocular vision tasks such as reading standard print letters — which can mask deficits caused by misalignment. This makes them more reliable when assessing monocular function under conditions where alignment issues interfere with normal perception.
The available literature supports superior performance of Landolt C in identifying subtle reductions in contrast-sensitive vision often seen early in strabismic cases compared to traditional Snellen-based assessments relying solely on letter recognition without considering orientation cues from gaps within shapes.

Therefore I conclude that yes – despite both being used clinically, their underlying design leads to meaningful functional distinctions especially regarding accuracy across varying degrees of ocular deviation present commonly among children diagnosed with strabismus-related poor sight development.\boxed{B}. Answer choice: C
